title: Brayan Bello excels in bulk relief, but opener melts in Red Sox’ 4-3 loss to Guardians
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Tyler Samaniego allowed four runs in the first inning in his opener debut against the Cleveland Guardians.
Brayan Bello excelled in bulk relief, pitching seven scoreless innings with five strikeouts and allowing no walks.
The Red Sox lost 4-3, placing them 10 games under .500 and last in the AL East division.

title: Giannis Antetokounmpo praises TD Garden environment amid trade rumors
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Giannis Antetokounmpo praised Celtics fans and TD Garden atmosphere during a recent livestream amid ongoing trade rumors.
Boston Celtics are not favorites to acquire Antetokounmpo, with Miami Heat reportedly having an edge in trade discussions.
Speculation involves possible trades including Jaylen Brown as a key piece in a potential deal for Antetokounmpo.

title: Harvard ‘Senior Sunrise’ cut short by cops
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Police cleared Harvard's Senior Sunrise gathering on Weeks Footbridge due to crowd concerns and swimming attempts.
The event involved hundreds from Harvard's Class of 2026 on the Charles River bridge in Cambridge.
Cambridge, Harvard University, and Massachusetts State Police responded; it is unclear if there were arrests or injuries.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Kill Bill: The Whole Bloody Affair' is now streaming on Peacock, featuring Uma Thurman's notable performance.
'The Truman Show,' starring Jim Carrey, is available on Paramount+ and YouTube.
'The Four Seasons' Season 2, featuring Tina Fey and Steve Carell, is currently streaming on Netflix.

title: Red Sox predicted to draft 18-year-old third baseman in 2026 MLB Draft by ESPN
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: ESPN's Kiley McDaniel predicts the Red Sox will draft Bo Lowrance, an 18-year-old third baseman, in the 2026 MLB Draft.
Lowrance, standing 6'5", 200 pounds, is compared to MLB's Freddie Freeman and is committed to the University of Virginia.
Lowrance may switch to first base due to position concerns, despite being lauded for his left-handed hitting abilities.

title: Lowell father and daughter charged in alleged payroll tax scheme
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Lim Ou and Nalen Ou of Lowell charged with tax crimes for allegedly concealing over $48 million in cash payroll.
The pair ran temporary employment agencies, paying workers under the table from 2019 to 2025 without reporting to the IRS.
They allegedly failed to pay over $12 million in federal employment taxes and face up to five years in prison if convicted.

title: Report: Christian Gonzalez unlikely to participate in OTAs under current Patriots contract
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Christian Gonzalez is absent from Patriots OTAs, seeking a contract extension for higher pay.
Gonzalez earned All-Pro and Pro Bowler honors but is playing under a $4 million rookie contract.
Patriots freed up cap space by restructuring Mike Onwenu's contract, potentially aiding Gonzalez's extension.

title: Search underway for driver who led cops on a chase through Everett, Malden, Stoneham
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A driver with multiple arrest warrants led police on a chase through Everett, Malden, and Stoneham.
The suspect's vehicle struck a State Police cruiser and nearly hit a Malden officer before being abandoned.
The suspect, wanted in connection with a drug investigation, is currently at large; police advise the public to report tips.

title: Ex-Patriots HC Jerod Mayo has a new job away from football
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jerod Mayo, former Patriots head coach, is now managing director at Fifth Down Capital, a private equity firm.
Mayo was fired after the Patriots' 2024 season ended with a 4-13 record, including two six-game losing streaks.
Fifth Down Capital, based in Needham, invests in technology, healthcare, and retail sectors, according to its website.

title: Mass. man reports beating Mount Everest speed climbing record
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Tyler Andrews, 36, from Concord, set a new speed record for climbing Mount Everest with supplemental oxygen.
Andrews completed the climb in 9 hours and 55 minutes, beating Lhakpa Gelu Sherpa's 2003 record by over an hour.
Nepal's authorities must verify the record, but Andrews's GPS tracking supports his claim of beating the previous record.
